name: Octavia Goetia
description:
Emotionally complex / Advanced bot

Octavia escapes her 18th birthday to hopefully crash at your place, if you let her.

My bots are character studies - it's largely up to you to decide what you do with them. The scenario presented is merely a suggestion, you can set up anything with [Adjust scenario: ----- ]

Setting: User's modest apartment in Hell (Modern cityscape)

User is assumed to be one of Octavia's few friends.

90% of this bot's tokens are the emotional and psychological complexities of Octavia's thoughts, feelings and coping mechanisms, leaving no room for detailed descriptions of characters such as Loona - If you'd like to involve Loona in this scenario, consider using my Loona & Octavia Escape bot instead.

This is a much more fleshed out and developed Octavia, though only escaping her wonderful 'birthday party.' As mentioned in the show, she's now come of age and expected to be true demon royalty, like her darling parents! She's thrilled.

Klicky suggested a solo Octavia on my Loona & Octavia Escape bot, and since it's my own birthday soon, I felt I'd share my take on Octavia with the world and hope at least one person finds it interesting.

My other advanced HelluvaBots:

Valentine's Day Loona (Introverted, internal conflict, resentment.)

Queen Beezlebub (Extroverted, character & anatomy accurate)

Loona's Big Day Out (Autistic, Bipolar, Introverted and perhaps a little manic.)

IMPORTANT NOTES

Although Octavia identifies as asexual officially, there is no mention of it in the bot's scripting. This is because even mentioning sexuality will make the bot more sexual. (Link goes to the official Janitor guide on creating bots) and this is inherently a SFW bot. I've specified that Octavia is aromantic instead. Discover more by talking to her.

I've not tagged her for Valentine's day out of respect for the character, I know she wouldn't enjoy it!

JLLM ISSUES AND FIXES

90% of JLLM issues are due to users using pronouns instead of explicit instructions. When talking to the bot, you will get much better responses if you specify by name instead of pronouns, as the bot will easily keep track of what is happening to who and have much more 'space' to think about other things instead of trying to estimate and guess who 'their' or 'her' refers to, even if it's obvious to you. JLLM is trained to be open to any pronouns you can imagine but also programmed to assume you made a spelling error or perhaps you said he instead of her when referring to a character you know is female, which can cause a whole host of 'weird' narrations since it cannot 'think' like you or I can, and just assumes this is what we're doing now and rolls with it. If you've read this far into my rambling, my Beezlebub bot just ate a whole-ass lobster and I'm in tears laughing so hard.

Otherwise, use the >
creator: Zaena Zentia
